description_long: This object is a futuristic, twin-boom airplane with a central fuselage and two large cylindrical engine nacelles mounted on either side. The nose is sharply pointed and features a cockpit canopy with multiple windows, suggesting a forward, enclosed cockpit. The wings are straight and connect the fuselage to the engine nacelles, with additional structural elements visible between the booms and the rear. The rear of the airplane features a distinctive H-tail configuration, with twin vertical stabilizers and horizontal stabilizers connecting the booms. The surface is primarily metallic gray with yellow and blue accents, and exposed mechanical details are visible along the engines and fuselage. The overall design is reminiscent of science fiction or advanced military aircraft, with a robust and detailed exterior.
description: A twin-boom, metallic gray airplane with a pointed nose, forward cockpit, and large cylindrical engines, featuring an H-tail and detailed mechanical elements.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pointed nose, forward cockpit canopy, and the twin cylindrical engine nacelles flanking the fuselage.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the elongated fuselage, cockpit placement, and the detailed structure of the engine nacelles and booms.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and the open structure of the engine nacelles.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the other profile of the fuselage, engines, and wing structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings connecting twin booms; no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted in nacelles on each boom.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and a horizontal stabilizer connecting the booms.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear with two main units and one nose gear (partially visible).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a multi-window canopy, likely multi-seat.
